candidates.why: Explicit total revenue line and equals Net sales in preview; valid top-line revenue label.
Direct sales/revenue line; also equals Total Revenue in preview and may be the primary reported revenue label in some years.

candidates.why: Direct COGS/cost of sales line item.

Possible COGS component, but not the main reported COGS line.

variables.notes: Selected the direct COGS line, not embedded components or balance sheet inventory rows.

candidates.why: Core SG&A operating overhead component.

Subset/breakout of Selling expenses, so less preferred than the main component row.

Amortization should be excluded from XSGA under the rules.

final_choice.row_label: Research & Development Expense in cost of goods sold

variables.notes: Explicit R&D line exists and should be used even though it is embedded in COGS.

candidates.why: Specific interest subcomponent rather than the main reported interest expense line.

Ideal generic label, but appears empty in the preview.

variables.notes: Using a priority list of the most semantically appropriate single reported interest-expense labels, with Financial expenses only as a fallback.

candidates.why: Preferred owner/parent equity line that excludes non-controlling interests.
Total equity includes non-controlling interests, so less preferred for BE when parent equity exists.
Balancing row, not an equity measure to select.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not an equity measure to select.
Whole balance sheet total, not book equity.

variables.notes: Selected parent equity to avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement profit attribution, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement comprehensive income attribution, not balance sheet minority equity.
Balance sheet minority interest line; valid fallback.
Best balance sheet label for minority/non-controlling equity.

variables.notes: Using balance sheet NCI labels only; income statement attribution rows are not appropriate for MIB.
